item.description: I really enjoyed reading Twitter during WWDC, tons of support for everyone and a general positivity. Now one week later, it’s back to passive-aggressive subtweeting and general hatred. Thanks, I’m back to just reading RSS and micro.blog.
SUVs are now just slightly taller normal cars, got it.
I just got an ad in the Outlook iOS App. Never deleted an App faster.
Inclusion in a Distributed World
Another submission for the public Loc Testflight. Let’s see.

Every project I start using the composable architecture comes to a grinding halt after a certain complexity is reached. I hope Apple will play a bigger role in defining the architecture for SwiftUI Apps.

Avoiding SwiftUI’s AnyView - Swift by Sundell

Very handy rules to keep in the back of your head when you are working with SwiftUI.
Internet 3.0 and the Beginning of (Tech) History - Stratechery
YAML frontmatter support for Zola just fills me with joy.
What have I done...

And the real SARS-CoV-2 shows up with the spiky Spike.

Reverse Engineering the source code of the BioNTech/Pfizer SARS-CoV-2 Vaccine

I’m living in Berlin now for more than 5 years and never heard about the “Schwerbelastungskörper”. Fascinating piece of history and a reminder about the horrific German past. http://www.schwerbelastungskoerper.de/the-information-center.html
On that note, the new A3 drives surprisingly well, but the lack of the arm rest I even worse in real life.

item.title: Every time I have a rental car, I dream about writing a car related blog. Maybe one day.

item.description: Looks like YAML frontmatter is coming to Zola! Time to migrate my old Jekyll websites soon. https://github.com/getzola/zola/pull/990
Renewed my iTunes Match subscription, like it’s 2011.

I’m spending less mindless time watching random videos on my phone nowadays. I think it’s related to the smaller screen of the Mini. I’m still watching Youtube videos, but only the ones from feeds I’m subscribed to using RSS. This happens always on the iPad.
I finally added basic docker compose support for my Twitter to RSS service. Time to host it on my Mini now as well. twitt_rss
My 11 Pro work phones feels huge! 100% satisfied with the 12 Mini choice so far.

I did not expect Rosetta 2 to be that performant. If Docker becomes available on M1 soon, I might consider replacing my Intel Mac Mini. Apple Silicon M1 Emulating x86 is Still Faster Than Every Other Mac in Single Core Benchmark
Feels like I will finally find time to focus on my private Apps again. So much todo. Especially for Icro and iOS14.
The iPhone 12 Mini size is perfect!
A morning without watching CNN and map-guy. Weird.
It seems to be over. What an election.
Following the election for 24h now. Tired.
I‘m giving self-hosted RSS aggregators another try. Hosting on your local server using Docker compose is so simple these days. FreshRSS looks very promising and is supported by Reeder. FreshRSS
Please, someone convince me to not write a macOS Markdown editor
With more and more 3rd party Apps updating, I’m starting to love the Big Sur look a lot!
We got an icon!
So close!
By now, I wish I could just create my App icons in SwiftUI and not deal with 100 different sizes.
Just finished migrating my 1Password to a self-hosted Bitwarden installation. One less thing in the cloud. bitwarden_rs
Obligatory IFTTT testpost.
Reeder 5 looks great. I will give it a shot for a few weeks as my default RSS reader. I also migrated all my read-later articles to it.
After a decade of storing bookmarks in read-later services, Pinboard, DEVONThink I noticed something: Storing bookmarks in the browser works as well. 🤦‍♂️
Not sure how but I missed the introduction of GroupBox in SwiftUI. Everything is a card now! GroupBox documentation
I missed that there was a new Unprofessional episode this year, still have great memories listening to it years ago. Unprofessional Podcast
Fell into a new rabbit hole about 3d printed model planes and cars just because of one channel: Tom Stanton Youtube
The App to post this landed on my homescreen. I should get started with the App icon.
It’s done. I migrated away from DEVONThink. Again.
I plan to use this like I used Twitter 10 years ago. Posting random thoughts and links. Let’s see!

item.title: Hello World

item.description: I recently bought a Fuji X100V after shooting years on iPhones only. I almost couldn’t believe how good and fun this camera is.
I felt more and more uneasy about the Fastmail server locations. Just migrated everything over to mailbox.org for half the price. It took 30 minutes. Own your domain!
Thank you all so much for the kind words and congratulations! It means so much to us! 🎉😭🥳
I was lucky, she said "Yes"!
Announcing Rust 1.45.0 | Rust Blog Can't wait to use stable Rust on all my Rocket services.

iOS 14 has been incredibly stable on my iPad and iPhone. Even while traveling and driving with CarPlay I did not experience a single issue. But for some reason the Big Sur update fried my main Mac Mini and it's currently in repair.

Unrelated feelings about the whole Hey situation:

I have 0 interest in another proprietary E-Mail host/App.

Own your domain and use public protocols.

App Store guidelines need to be just and mutual beneficial. The playing field should be as even as possible.

Side-loading of notarized Apps could drive new innovation on many areas.

Apple, why are you fighting this battle now?
Remember me talking about using Xcode on the iPad? I have been coding like this the past week and really enjoy it. It's macOS in a box! Good latency and retina resolution with Jump Desktop.
I’m not sure how I missed the Rustlings repo when I was starting out with Rust. Just finished all the exercises, still fun to do later on!

Oh no.

‘WUT?!’ INDEED
I hope more companies reflect on their Facebook SDK usage and remove it. It always has been a tech and privacy concern, now the tech concern is very visible.
Let's give this little guy more Ram.

I’m reintroducing the cell swipe-actions in the latest Icro beta. I use the iOS 13 long-press context menu wherever possible and overestimated the general adoption. I hope iOS 14 brings speed and discoverability improvements to the context menus to drive the adoption.
Oh no. I hadn't considered that before getting into vim.
Icro 2.2 for iOS and macOS is now available in the App Stores. 8h from submission to release for both platforms. 🙌
The release of the Magic Keyboard and mouse pointer support really pushes iPad Apps forward. Context menus everywhere! 😍

In my full-time job, I'm working on a project that was started almost 12 years ago. I have no illusion that I would be able to compile and run this on Xcode for iPad. In my spare time, I'm building a pure SwiftUI App with a few external SwiftPM dependencies, no custom build scripts besides SwiftLint, no interface builder. I don't see why it wouldn't be great to work on this on the iPad.

I don't understand the negative sentiment towards Xcode on iPad. Even comments like: "it will only be used by children to ship their first toy App". Wouldn't this be already a great achievement in itself?
After listening to a recent ATP, I finally installed Pi-hole on my Mac Mini Server. Having blocked ads without shady extensions is magic. Pi-hole
I declare Easter as my password hygiene holiday. Deleted 400 entries from my 1Password store!
My girlfriend recently got a Switch and Animal Crossing. She would love if someone comes by her island: Dodo Code L1TN0 It's her second day only!
I finally finished rewriting my hacky Jekyll micropub endpoints in Rust. Now hosted for free on Heroku and GitHub pages.
Something is missing...
If Apple is going to announce the ARM transition soon, maybe we can install macOS on this new iPad? 😁
Internal SwiftPM packages are a game changer for me. It‘s finally as easy as it should be to modularize an App.

Graphical depiction of ownership and borrowing in Rust - Rufflewind's Scratchpad

Spending way too little time on writing Rust, this still is a helpful guide.

A German newspaper writing about RSS, not sure why now but spreading awareness is great!

Schnüren Sie sich Ihr persönliches Nachrichtenpaket - Berliner Morgenpost

The Tragic iPad – Stratechery by Ben Thompson

"Apple set the standard that highly complex, innovative software that was only possible on the iPad could only ever earn 5 bucks from a customer forever (updates, of course, were free)."
A new Icro iOS Testflight build is available including support for fetching a micropub access token using Indieauth. I wish there was offical Testflight for Mac support!
Don't mind me, just testing my IndieAuth flow.
My first PR towards NetNewsWire just got merged. I love the App and the whole open-source project!
Finally, basic Markdown highlighting in the upcoming Icro beta.
Waiting for The Incomparable Star Wars podcasts - A modern Christmas tradition
Hiroshima
Tea ceremony (Kyoto), Naoshima
Kyoto 🍁
I missed this place. Tokyo.
Lisbon
Icro 2.1 is now live on both App stores: Mac | iOS
Double review! Finally managed to submit Icro for Mac as well.
Icro 2.1 with basic support for push notifications is now waiting for review!
Tate Modern - Olafur Eliasson: In Real Life
It seems like the experimental push support for Icro is working! I might be able to ship it with a few smaller adjustments.
Now with Catalina out, here is what I call Beta 1 of Icro for Mac: Download. It’s very far from perfect or bug free, but I believe it’s usable.
I hear more people having issues with their Watch Series 5 battery live: After 3-4 days in use, the battery is totally on par with my series 4. The first few days were bad though, no idea why.
Icro 2.0 is finally waiting for review. Let‘s see when Catalina is released to finish the macOS version.
Finally, the latest macOS Catalina Beta and Xcode 11 GM support SwiftUI in Catalyst Apps again. I can continue with Icro on the Mac 🙌
I submitted the internal Icro frameworks to the Swift Source Compatibility Suite. Because of this I already merged the Xcode11 changes to master and finally removed CocoaPods.
Icro for macCatalyst update: It starts to look like a macOS App. The sidebar will come to the iPad version, too. It’s just nicer to use then the bottom bar.
“Code that isn’t integrated into your team’s mainline branch should be considered a liability at best, and dead at worst.” Continuous Integration
Grossglockner High Alpine Road
Icro for Mac stays on hold until the Xcode betas support Catalyst Apps with SwiftUI again. 🙁
Finally some mountains outside my window.
So close.
What happens when you combine Flappy Bird and PUBG... Flappy Royal
My bank finally supports Apple Pay, welcome to 2014.
As with everything related to Icro, the changes made to support Catalyst and iOS 11 are open-source in the Xcode 11 branch. Please don’t judge my hacks to work with Beta 1 APIs. 😁
For the brave ones: Catalyst version of Icro. Requires macOS 10.15, many many things are broken. Download
macOS App notarization was surprisingly quick and easy. iOS App signing was a strict teacher.
Is someone already running Catalina and interested in trying something out? Might need a few more days but getting closer!
I quickly open-sourced a typed wrapper around UIImage(systemName:) as I need it for Icro. Maybe someone else has a use for it. 🤷‍♂️ TypedSymbs
I‘m currently exploring and writing a short blogpost about Icro and iOS13. Many changes ahead! I almost can‘t remember the last time there was so much excitement in the iOS community.
I can‘t stop calling it Marzipan.
Here is the official Apple WWDC playlist: WWDC 2019 - Playlist
So many nits!
Wow, Audio sharing!
Dmn, they know their audience. For all mankind!
Let’s go!
Now you should also see the WWDC Micro.blog discovery category in Icro. Might require a restart.
I‘m looking forward to the shared WWDC music playlists!
One day before the WWDC keynote is a perfect time to start building a declarative UI framework for settings screens, right? Right?!
Was able to buy two tickets to the Talk Show for colleagues; I’m not even going myself. Talk Show tickets as a service.
The latest Icro testflight crashes when opening images. I’ll provide a fix during the weekend. 👍

Chrome to limit full ad blocking extensions to enterprise users - 9to5Google

This was inevitable with Google’s business model. Safari is still great, also Firefox is getting better again. 🤷‍♂️
I’m experimenting with Micro.blog crossposting again. First to Mastodon, maybe Twitter later?

Alfred 4 Is Here! - Alfred Blog

Always one of the easiest update decisions for me.
First day at UIKonf 2019

I started some bigger refactorings in the Icro codebase and open-sourced a tiny helper: AppDelegateComponent to help declutter AppDelegate. Definitely not my idea but I reimplemented it in almost every App I work on.
I needed to unsubscribe from the Polygon RSS feed as they constantly spoil GoT and Movie things. Just in the headline. Don‘t assume everyone lives in your timezone.
I‘m sad to miss IndieWebCamp Berlin again. Have fun everyone!
I love Italy...and Aperol Spritz.
My Music player App in development is currently built on Realm. The MongoDB acquisition makes me uneasy and I‘ll most likely convert it to good old Core Data.
Hello Florence!
With all the rumors I can’t wait for WWDC now. Marzipan and the iPad improvements sound amazing.
Icro 1.3.2 with video playback is now submitted. In the meantime I automated the screenshot creation with Fastlane. I should have done it way sooner.

The latest Icro TestFlight includes basic playback for videos hosted on Micro.blog.

If you want to be part of the TestFlight group, sign up here
Super excited to finally try out the ECG feature on my Apple Watch. If only the update process was faster.

iOS App Analytics a Necessary Evil, or Maybe Just an Evil

It almost hurts how true this is. I don’t use any tracking for my private Apps but I have to deal with it a lot in my day job.

Semantic Line Breaks

This is something I try to do for my personal notes already. It’s great seeing it formalized like this.
Last weekend I had a BMW M140 as a rental. I really miss this ridiculous car.
Good luck to everyone trying to get WWDC ticket. May the odds be ever in your favor. Sadly I‘m not planning to go this year.

The Music App needs 1-N communication in a few places. I decided to go with this super lightweight reactive library I discovered. It’s very pleasant to use! RuiAAPeres/Receiver: Swift µframework implementing the Observer pattern 📡
I’m very happy with the progress of my music player. The scope is only offline playback and being able to import music from network drives or the Files.app.
I started working on a new App, an offline first Music player on iOS. As always, I love to work on Apps that I want to use myself.
Wow, the performance increase is shocking, I should have done this way sooner.
I moved all my sites and services to it’s own VPS, finally away from the shared hosting.
Thank you all so much for the support through the Tip Jar! 😮
After listening to the latest Reconcilable Differences I finally started reading Michael Lopp's books. I'm hooked!
Another quick test.
The Tip Jar is now live in Icro 1.3.1 :)
It seems like there was an issue getting the in app purchases for the tip jar through App review. I’m on it.
Icro 1.3 with themes, a sharing extension for URLs and a Tip Jar should be in the App Store soon! 🎉

iOS Dev Directory

Don’t mind me, I’m just doing some final Icro 1.3 tests.
I open-sourced my very first piece of Rust code. It‘s a tiny server to convert a Twitter list stream into RSS. hartlco/twitt_rss
Our first british Sunday roast at The Old Queens Head.

Advanced UIView shadow effects using shadowPath – Hacking with Swift

I always need to remind myself to define the shadow path whenever possible.
Weekend trip to London. I enjoy this city now way more than 7 years ago when I visited the last time

I deleted the Youtube.app from all my devices. Too often I was just watching randomly recommended videos and not the videos I’m really interested in. I added all my subscriptions to my RSS reader and now download the videos I want to see directly with a Siri Shortcut.
Of course I already discovered issues in the build, but that’s what Betas are for...

I submitted the first TestFlight Beta for Icro 1.3 including the themes and the share extension. I hope it will be available for public testing early next week. Here is a screenshot of the dark gray theme:
iOS developers remember: Run you App in Christmas mode (Color blended layers) to spot easy scroll performance improvements.
For Icro 1.3 I’m working on a share sheet for text and URLs and themes (3 for now). I hope to ship it in January.
Mast is a nice Mastodon client. I started using it yesterday and really enjoy it so far!
I started working on a sharing extension for Icro (links and text first). To prevent code duplication I’m moving a lot UI code into another framework. Not as easy as expected!

I just noticed I didn’t buy much tech stuff this year. My favorite is the used iPod from 2006 I bought on eBay. Even if the I would have bought the iPhone XS Max and new Apple Watch, the iPod would still be my favorite.
I wanted to learn Go but quickly noticed how much I miss real enums with associated values, generics and optionals on the language level. I'm now looking into Rust.
Icro 1.2 should be out soon!
I just noticed: I can do all my go development work on iOS with Filebrowser, Prompt and Textastic.
After this I want to focus again on Icro for macOS and rewrite my server code in Go.
We are getting closer to the Icro 1.2 release. I‘m aiming to submit within the next 3 days.
It was successful and I hope it will last another one or two years.

I was really close to buying a new Mac Mini yesterday. Instead I did one last upgrade to my 2012 Mac Mini: Replaced the built in spinning disk with a SSD, so it no longer uses my Samsung T3 as a boot drive. Of course the hard drive was in the lower bay.
SourceKit LSP was very easy to setup in Sublime Text. I'm excited where this is going!
Yesterday was the first time I used Waze over CarPlay. What a great experience. CarPlay now would be a must-have for any car I buy.
Everyone ordered a new iPad and I bought an iPod Video 5.5 on eBay. I‘m tired of streaming services and giving away control over the music I own.

Thanks to jet lag I had some extra coding time for Icro for iOS. I finally merged the Icon update pull request and implemented emoji collections in the discover tab.

You should be able to get it from TestFlight now 👍
Back home in cold, rainy Berlin. I‘m already missing Japan.
We went to our first Korean BBQ. The beef was unbelievable.

I noticed the only Twitter content I'm actually interested in is iOS/Mac development related. I subscribed now again to a tiny private Twitter list in Feedbin. It's just another feed in my RSS reader now!
I hope you are not tired of Japan pictures yet. We arrived in Osaka!
Saw the iPhone XR today. Loved the form factor. Definitely the phone to recommend to non-techy friends. Huge crowds around the phones in the Osaka Apple Store.
Be aware, they will steal all your peanuts. In the first picture, the humans are behind the bars, not the monkeys. 🐒
Be aware, they will steal all your cookies.
I love the houses here.
By the way, I can‘t believe how much I love the iPhone XS Max camera. We left our DSLR at home to travel as light as possible and I didn‘t regret it once.
First full day in Kyoto!
Senso-ji Temple
Tokyo!
There is an open pull request to Icro for updating the Icons. I'm still not decided and would love to get feedback! Pull request
I enjoy seeing all those Watch Face App prototypes. I miss seeing great ideas for digital faces as I prefer a digital clock on displays.
Finally got my iPhone XS Max. It’s the first big big phone for me. I love the bigger keyboard 😅
Ok 👌
If this works, we have the first post from Icro for Mac!
I‘m also not sure if this is a good idea.
You can now join the Icro TestFlight yourself: Link
Icro 1.1.1 is finally waiting for review. 🤞
The new Icro Testflight should be out now. If everything goes well, I plan to submit tomorrow. This will be the first release with code from other contributors!
How ironic, I messed up the link: Buy me a Coffee

I closed my Patreon creators page. It was too confusing for me so see when someone will get charged. I think none of my Patrons got charged got charged in the end!

If you are still interested in supporting me, go here instead: Buy me a coffee
I just submitted the Xcode 10 Icro build for Testflight review. 🖖🏻
Can’t wait for this iOS beta cycle to be over. The constant Xcode switching drives me crazy.
Oh no, the “I should learn Haskell” thought is back in my head.
And the final test from within Icro.
Seems like it worked, time for deployment.
Testing my new micropub endpoint
Moved the Twitter and Mastodon App into a folder on my phone. That’s probably healthy.
Didn’t take long for my Mastodon timeline to become toxic.
Icro is the #4 trending Swift repo today! That’s a first for me. GitHub Swift trending
Icro is now open-source! Github
Seeing GitHawk doing the open source iOS approach gives me hopes for open source Icro.
Open-sourcing Icro will be a long term project. And I’m still not sure how to prevent exact duplicates from popping up in the App Store.
Munich once a year is great but I’m happy to go back to Berlin.
One step closer to open-sourcing Icro: Added SwiftLint and fixed hundreds of warnings. Should have done this from the beginning but development was a rush.
Oh, not to forget: A favorite action button 😅
Icro 1.1 got approved. I hope it shows up soon. Major new feature is the image uploading to micropub media endpoints; including Micro.blog.
Icro 1.1 is waiting for review. 🤞
Anyway, new TestFlight beta is out and a few new testers are registered. Thank you for helping out!
Seems like I can‘t submit Icro 1.1 today as the Licensing and Tax module in iTunes connect is in maintenance mode and I need to sign a new agreement. 😅
Many people in my Twitter timeline are starting to use alternative platforms. Too few are considering a platform where you own and control your content.
Wow, I have been quiet recently. This summer is too crazy.
Uff
Back in Berlin from our summer vacation. Next big vacation will be Japan in October, I‘m so excited!
I still love it here!
Lake Garda
I almost already had a week of vacation and still 0 mails in my inbox Filters and rules work as planned 👌
Downloaded my first hiking App, it‘s getting serious.
Innsbruck is lovely!
I finally lucked out in the rental car game again.
Finally no mails again 👌
I wasn’t there from the very first day, but close:
Forgot to post my obligatory “dumpling time” photo!
Of course Apple translated the name of the “Shortcuts” app. But „Kurzbefehle“ sounds clunky.
I added a „Photos“ filter into the timeline in Icro. I really love browsing this stream. A new Testflight with an alpha of micropub image upload and the photos section should be ready soon.
I finally started importing old Instagram photos to my site. Instagram and the whole internet was a different place back than. 😕
How have I missed the new Now, Now album?

My first thought about Instagram TV: Wow, another platform where you don’t really control the distribution of your content. My second thought: There is a reason why video evolved into wide screen landscape...

I really don’t get this feature.
Scroll performance has greatly improved in iOS 12 Beta 2! I love it.

I was working on some scroll performance improvements for Icro today. To reach 60fps constantly I‘ll probably need to go one level lower than UILabel and attributed strings. That‘s something I really wanted to avoid.
For the curious ones: The image uploading interface will be rather simple and look like this:
I really want to play with the Marzipan hacks now. So curious how Icro will look.
Goodbye San Jose, it was a great week. Hope to see you soon!
Good morning San Jose.
The Talk Show
The Talk Show live was a blast, as always!
Second WWDC day and I still didn‘t install a beta OS. It‘s getting harder 😂
The important question: Has someone seen the Odwalla fridge?
This years WWDC playlist is great so far!
Queuing time:
Finally
Hey San Jose, why is it so hot?
Let's go!
Be prepared for many stupid pictures in the next week. I’m cross-posting my Instagram story.
It's time!
Lufthansa has 4 flights from Berlin Tegel to Frankfurt within an hour. Berlin being an hub for a big airline would have been so nice.
My evening activities for WWDC are set: I’ll be at the ATP, Talk Show and Relay.fm recording.
The last two weeks I was very quiet about Icro development. Truth is there was just too much to do in my jobby job. I should have a lot more energy and time to finish image uploading after WWDC! 🙂

I just gave Omnifocus 3 a test run. I think I’ll stick with Things. The contextual headings in projects became a crucial part of organizing my tasks. Also I still don’t like the design and interaction with the “homepage” in OF 2/3.
Seeing people leave for San Jose gets me excited. Can‘t wait for my flight on Sunday.
Solo was the first of the new era Star Wars movie that I have not seen in the midnight premiere. I really regret it now, for obvious reasons.
In one week I’m already on my way to San Jose. Time is flying!
It’s great to be back in Amsterdam, even for just one night!
As Instapaper is temporarily no longer available in Europe, I abandoned it all together. I‘m using Pinboards “unread” feature now. Pinner on iOS is still great.
Icro 1.0.2 was just approved. It should show up sooner or later in your App Store :)
I was pretty quiet here recently. Icro 1.0.2 is in App review and I already started the work on image posting. WWDC is coming up in 2 weeks. Busy times ahead!
I’m sitting in the Ikea restaurant while my girlfriend watches the royal wedding livestream. 🙄

I'm currently uploading a new Icro beta, I hope it's available soon to all the testers! It switches micropub Auth header to "Bearer" and more smaller dynamic type improvements. There is way more to do in upcoming versions.
Half a year ago, the latest Twitter announcements would have annoyed me a lot. Now with a great community on micro.blog I almost do not care anymore.
I really cannot wait for the new version of Ulysses, especially the new code blocks.
Related: My horrible Swift script to convert the Day One json export into .textbundle files is still working. Github
I really value how open Day One is about their recent cloud sync issue but I think I’m switching back to my offline external folder in Ulysses solution.
Just ordered my US prepaid sim for WWDC in a few weeks. Having a connection the moment you land makes traveling way easier.
I just bought 1Keyboard to type on my iPhone/iPad using my Mac keyboard. This is super handy!
Thank you all so much for the feedback and the support. This community here really is special!

My Patreon is now ready. If you really insist on supporting me, feel free to use it. It's not a monthly fee, I will couple it to major milestone releases and will notify Patreons at least a week beforehand.
First custom micropub test from within Icro.
Of course, all this is to add general micropub support to Icro.
Oh wow, this was way easier than expected.
Quick test for my micro pub endpoint
Thank you all so much for the awesome feedback. I’m still going through it. Tomorrow I’ll start working on new features!
Icro is finally live! I can’t believe it 😅
On completely different note: I cooked an incredible steak with a nice pan sauce today: The hours watching Binging with Babish paid off.
So far no response from Apple. The big time zone difference also doesn’t make things easier.

It’s already late in the evening in Germany and still no sign of Icro on the App Store. I hope to wake up to an answer from the iTunes Connect team and an available App. Another lesson learned: Be quiet about the App status until it’s really visible inside the store.

Icro will be free but it’s still not showing up in the App Store. For some reason using a promo code enables the download: 7P49YKP9NMXK X4LMRRP363XF X7HHY4WYHX9E TKLLJ6XPTYLF 4MK9LPFJTMLL WYEEW3KYKEAE XAHPKR67RJNA WFJM7TPPNHHN 6EERAX44FNYM YYRTFFHMPA9L
The App Store rollout is incredibly slow today. This is supposed to be the Link: Icro
Oh my, it’s finally processing!
The 2017 Panic Report I love this company and their products!
Today I worked on GDPR compliance for my older projects. All I did was making sure that I no longer use any analytics or crash reporting libraries.
While the 1.0 is still blocked by App review, I added a nice improvement to the compose window: Show the post you are answering to.
Icro 1.0 was rejected because of problems with the user generated content guidelines and many questions about pricing of the service (micro.blog) Fun times ahead.
The just published Icro Testflight version is planned to be the last one before the initial release.
Yesterday was our anniversary. 8 years pass quickly.
A new Testflight build is processing. I can‘t wait now to finalize 1.0 and start working on new feature updates!
06:52am is too early to wonder how to correctly calculate the size of an utf8 String with emojis.
My 3 most used Apps now rely on open web standards: Podcasts with Overcast, RSS using lire and my micro.blog client.
I plan to submit a new Testflight build tonight. In the meantime, I added the people that signed up later to the initial build. Happy testing.
This morning I worked on rendering the custom Wordpress emojis as inline images instead of showing them inside the image gallery. Thank you @manton for the suggestion!
The first feedback for Icro has been great. Thank you all for testing!

On more note about the Icro beta: This build contains the Hockey crash logging SDK. It just makes it faster to collect and identify crashes. I will remove the SDK when I ship the final version. Just to have a smaller binary in the end.
First Icro Testflight invites are out!
And actually, it’s always a great learning opportunity to try out new libraries!

Just a general note about Icro: During development of version 1.0, I heavily relied on 3rd party libraries. This is just due to time reasons. I’m working full-time as an iOS engineer and there is only a small amount of time and motivation left to work on side projects.
I think now I'm spending more time on micro.blog than on Twitter. That's a great change.
I think the whole migration went well!
I'm migrating my micro posts from my subdomain to my main site. I hope U don't duplicate my whole feed by accident.
I think I want to merge micro.hartl.co and this site again. I enjoy microblogging so much and here is no content...
First Icro Testflight build is submitted for review. Should take around a day to get approved.
Sorry for the spam 😅
You can now sign up for the Icro beta test: Sign up here
I just saw Lady Bird and loved it. So far my favorite movie I saw this year.
This is the first build without any hardcoded usernames or passwords! Exciting!
Quick test for the new WordPress config
Posting to micro.blog hosted sites is working now. Now I’m focusing on the settings screen with WordPress blog setup and logout. All that’s left on my 1.0 roadmap are then only better error messages.

Before working on Icro, I spent some time building a macOS markdown editor. The rumors about an upcoming cross-platform UI framework for macOS and iOS somehow killed my motivation. Maybe I’ll come back to it when the framework really is released.
Babysteps again, login with mail and access token is working now. Next steps are logout, posting to micro.blog accounts and WordPress setup.

Here is the quick video showing scrolling in Icro. Compression on Vimeo is kind of bad, but you’ll at least see a functioning App.

https://vimeo.com/265446974
Later this evening I’m panning to do a small screencast of the current state.
Todays Icro progress: – Share functionality – Better cache handling – Small cell design tweaks – Loading states in all parts of the App
On the top left is the unread indicator. You could say I was inspired by my favorite App, Tweetbot.
Icro now cashes the timeline and discover response and keeps a history up to 400 posts per stream. It also saves the read position. I love working on this project!
This morning I implemented replying in Icro. What’s left now is proper login, wordpress setup and posting with micro.blog accounts. 2 weeks till the first beta!
I can imagine a lot of people will not be happy about the color choices in Icro. But you know what? I’m tired of building blue, green or black Apps.

I‘m cutting more very basic features for 1.0 of Icro. No image posting, no favoriting interactions. No deleting of posts. With this I hope to have a very quick beta round and definitely release before WWDC.
Todays Icro progress: A basic list showing followed users.
Today I finally implemented follow functionality in Icro. Babysteps.

I have finally reached a scroll performance for my micro.blog client that I’m happy with. Biggest bottlenecks were NSAttributedStrings rendered with TTTAttributedLabel (replaced by plain UILabel with custom link touch detection) and displaying big images in small UIImageViews (solved by resizing images in background thread and caching).
I expected the worst after seeing the trailer for Ready Player One, but I really enjoyed the whole movie!
I spent the whole morning tweaking scroll performance for Icro, my micro.blog App. Still not there where I want to be but got way closer.
Star Wars In Concert was great. Totally worth the money.
Excited!
I’m now on my way to see Star Wars A New Hope in Concert. Pretty exciting.

I’ve been using the latest generation of 15” MacBook Pros for almost half a year. It’s a good machine but I’m happy that it’s a work machine and I don’t own it myself. The possibility of broken keys would drive me crazy.
Feels like iOS 3 times: The scroll performance bottleneck was date formatting.
I’m considering switching back to UITableViews for my micro.blog client. Somehow the scroll performance is way smoother with tables.
I’m super happy that 1Password continues to offer the single license. I just upgraded to version 7, easiest 40 bucks spent ever.
Any here with experience with the micro.blog API? I’m unable to get older posts than the last 36 from /posts/all. Every combination with before_id returns an empty array.
@manton Is there a way to get more than the last 36 items from the feed using /posts/all? The API returns empty items when I use before_id=“ID of the last post from /posts/all”.
My prototype can now replace the official micro.blog App for me. I think I’m actually going to release this!
And that’s the first test from my App
Sorry, that’s an XMLRPC test.

I just implemented a conversation view and profile tab. What’s missing:

  • Login
  • Logut
  • Posting
    • Hosted blogs
    • XML-RPC compatible blogs
    • Replying
    • Image upload
  • Pagination
  • Deleting posts
  • Faving
  • Design adjustments
    • Better cell layout with date
    • Galleries
  • App icon
I dug out my old micro.blog client project to try out a new design pattern. It already is in a better state than expected. Maybe I should continue working on it.
Will there be a micro.blog meetup at WWDC this year?
The Git Tower 3 beta is glorious. Editing commit messages? One click. Squashing and reordering of commits? Drag and drop!
I did not really expect it but: See you in San Jose!
First Aid Kit
Uff…
I could not care less about Vero: https://twitter.com/pasql/status/968168493521784832
Bright
Hey Instagram, I‘m just looking at images. There is no need to pause my audio session.
Weekly #14
Even though I needed to wait over a week for an Apple Store appointment, the actual service was great. Got a new phone as a replacement.
Just finished my first run of the year. I need to make this habit!
Berlin really needs an second Apple Store. The earliest genius bar appointment is in 7+ days.
I started using soke time tracking tools in my work and personal life. So far I like the idea of intentional focus shifts.
This bottle Four Roses Single Barrel is incredible. You can have phneomenal Bourbon for under 30€ a bottle. And it’s shipped to you from Amazon. 🥃
iOS Beta season came early.
Weekly #12
There are tons of Ikea boxes waiting to be assembled today.
Let‘s see how posting from Micron works.
I‘m looking for a service to pipe my twitter timeline into an RSS-feed. I‘m very close to write something like this in Ruby.
Short Weekly #11 is out.
I’m now slowly importing more and more old Instagram pictures to the micro blog. I love it!
Ah, just noticed that this year we will get Xcode X.
No more iPods that don’t run iOS. This makes me kind of sad. The iPod was the product the got me interested in Apple.
Weekly #10 is up!
I started playing The Last of Us Remastered on my Playstation. It’s really great so far, can’t wait to continue.
Yesterday I opened my 2012 Mac Mini for the first time to replace ram. What a beautiful and functional design! Unplugging the machine took longer than replacing the ram.
I’m having a holdiay party here with my nice Nikka whiskey.
I decided against cross-posting my content here to Twitter. People should use micro.blog itself or RSS to read micro blog content.
It looks like we are travelling to Japan in September of this year. This is going the be exciting.
What is up, my stream feels verry active this evening!
It seems like DejaLu, my favourite macOS mail client, is now on Github.
My 2017 product toplist.
Today Time Machine helped me a ton to restore overwritten Jenkins config files but…oh my god…to whole UI and workflow is still horrible.
I‘m now toying with the idea in my head to import my whole Twitter and Instagram archive to my micro blog. It would be a pretty big operation but i I would regain ownership over my content…
My last Weekly post for the year is up!
I just discovered NSFilePresenter while working on my small markdown App. Foundation is just awesome.
People should not wear noise canceling headphones in public spaces…especially supermarkets.
After some internal struggles, I switched back the main site to Jekyll. Kirby was great but had no real upsides for me.
I did a lot of small style tweaks on the main site today. Still a lot left to do.
MarsEdit almost makes me want to use WordPress again.
Christmas, or the time of the year when I can‘t catch up to my podcasts.
Super hot take: The iPhone X in the leather case is way more slipier that without a case.
How did I miss the beautiful RSS-App on iOS? lire Awesome native desgin, great functionality!
This is exciting but I’m not sure how this will look like in the end.
The black mode in Twitterrific on the iPhone X is glorious. I‘m happy I gave it another try thanks to the App Santa sale.
I don’t know why, but my post appeared on the Discover tab on micro.blog. 👍
Finally some syntax highlighting in my little side project.
I can’t believe how much I enjoy macOS development coming from iOS. Tuning shortcuts and workflows to increase usage speed is so rewarding.
I was wondering if I can publish with Ulysses?
I‘m just sitting here and hoping for a special episode to come out.
Out of the cinema. Let‘s now wait a few days until all the Star Wars podcasts are released…
9 hours to go until the premiere!
This iMac Pro looks great but there is absolutely no reason for me to get it.
Weekly #6 – Notch edition
People that are bothered by ads in Facebook messenger: What did you expect to happend??
Can anyone tell me if month 13 is out of bounds?
In 7 days I will already have seen the new Star Wars movie. It‘s getting exciting.
I just started using the MarsEdit beta and now it‘s released already. Can‘t wait to test it more!
This Stackoverflow answer is still the one I visit regularly.
I had to put a case around the iPhone X. Slightly too big for my hands to have a secure grip.
Got my iPhone X today. Restoring from iCloud backup including automatically transferring the Apple Watch setting was flawless.
I just posted Weekly #5 on the main page: https://hartl.co/blog/weekly-5
First advent sunday in Berlin and it‘s snowing!
By accident I started working on a plain-text editor App for macOS. I hope to bring a first release to Github today.
HTTPS certificate is now also in place. Good to go!
Let’s try pinging micro.blog on a new post.
Ok, let’s do this micro blogging again. This time proper with a dedicated site! 🙂
It seems like Amazon discontinued the unlimited Drive in the US. It‘s still running in Germany but not for too long, I guess. Now is the time to slowly migrate my Arq backup to Backblaze B2 🙂

Brockdorff Klang Labor…eine Band die ich bestimmt schon seit 8 Jahren nicht mehr gehört habe. Durch Apple Music habe ich heute aber entdeckt, dass sie 2012 ein neues Album herausbrachten. Das höre ich nun seit Stunden in Dauerschleife.
Mein Day One Ersatz ist mittlerweile eine Markdown-Datei, die nach und nach in einen privaten Jekyll-Blog exportiert wird.
Es ist wohl an der Zeit DayOne zu ersetzen. Werde zu einem privaten Markdown-dateibasierenden Jekyll System wechseln.
Nach einem Jahr sind jetzt alle Einträge von der alten Seite auch endlich hier. Hatte total vergessen, dass ich die Migration nie abgeschlossen hatte.
Warum heißt mein W-Lan nicht Router 66?
Mir ist eben aufgefallen, dass mein Publish-Workflow einfacher und schneller auf iOS als auf dem Mac ist.
Wenn man deutschen Wetterdiensten glauben darf, ist Regen mit dem bedrohlichsten Unwetter gleichzusetzen. Die Schulen und Ämter bleiben geschlossen, es regnet!
Die neue Freundesliste in Apple Music ist das erste social feature von Apple das Sinn macht!
S-Bahn train ride
Könnt ihr euch noch erinnern als color key Fotos gut aussahen? Gut, ich mich auch nicht.
Ein bisschen Weltuntergangsstimmung gestern Nacht.
Wenn ich nach meinem Facebook-Feed gehe, glauben Leute alles wenn es mit den Worten “Laut einer Studie” beginnt.
iOS 11 Beta 2 fühlt sich zumindest auf dem iPhone schon erstaunlich stabil und schnell an.
Scheinbar war gestern doch kein guter Tag für neue Betas, wie wäre es dann mit heute?
Seit gestern habe ich watchOS 4 installiert und benutze das Siri-Watchface. Nach ein wenig Konfiguration bin ich sehr davon angetan!
Heute wäre doch ein guter Tag für iOS 11 Beta 2.
The new Lorde Album [Melodrama](https://itun.es/de/oudlib) is finally out. I was waiting for 4 year for this. Also got tickes for the show in Berlin!
Sunrise in San Jose
The Talk Show Live at WWDC San Jose
Auf dem Weg nach San Jose zur WWDC. Das erste Flugzeug ist dazu noch ein A320neo. Das erste Mal für mich in diesem Flugzeug.
Keine Woche mehr bis zur WWDC!
I have the feeling my iPad Pro experiment is over. I do a lot of work on iOS, but it’s mainly on the iPhone.
I have been using Things 3 for the past month and still love it. Easiest buying decision in a while.
I’m back to using Newsify for my casual news consumptions. The information density just was too low on Flipboard.
You are wondering how is buying Star Wars themed parfume? Yes, me!
This workflow I was talking about is a variation of the one mentioned [here](http://www.hardscrabble.net/2015/how-to-jekyll-from-ios/).
This new workflow to post from Editorial is now clearly the fastest way to publish on my blog!
Testing a new Workflow to post directly from Editorial without Git client.
Wow, the flow to commit and push in on go is very streamlined. [Working Copy](https://workingcopyapp.com) looks impressive so far!
I’m now trying the Working Copy Git Client App on iPhone. I’m afraid the development stopped for Git2Go.
I decided against creating a separate blog for micro posts. I’m not posting that much anyway and it’s easier to manage.
@manton Do you think it’s too early for 3rd party clients? I started working on a custom client for iPhone and iPad.
New episodes of ATP, Hello Internet and Do by Friday. Great start into the weekend.
The hype about the AirPods is legit! I immediatelly fell in love with mine. Double tap to play/pause works better for me than the old EarPods remote.
A day into Micro.blog and already I want to build a third party client. Maybe this is the perfect opportunity for a React Native project.
All of a sudden I’m very interested in AirPods as Overcast 3.1 adds standalone Apple Watch playback. I just don’t understand why the shipping time still is 6 weeks.
My favorite texteditor from years ago, [Chocolat](https://chocolatapp.com/), recently received some updates. I’m very tempted to give it another shot. 😂
Back to English: I moved all my text based Workflow.app workflows back to Editorial. Feels more futureproof at the moment.
All meine Blog-Workflows wechsel ich jetzt zu Editorial. Die Zukunft von Workflow.app ist mir zu ungewiss und es ist eh alles textbasiert.
Habe heute das erste Mal Kaffee mit einer Moka-Kanne gemacht. Nichts ist explodiert oder verbrannt!
Let's see if my mobile publishing workflow still works.
Oh, a new The xx single! And a new album in January.
I miss being angry about MacBooks.
The Playstation 4 is now the first console I owned after the SNES. Let's see if the graphics did improve.
I'm still nervous while using my phone in the rain, even though Apple is promoting it in a terrible ad.
I'm back to iA Writer as my iOS plain text editor. It's one of the few big players that receives regular updates.
The new 13" MacBook Pro is really tempting. 🤑
I'm still waiting for you, Mac Mini!
The jetblack phone is really grippy, completely different to the 6/6s.
iPhone 7 Jet Black it is!
I’m slowly migrating away from Dropbox. 1Password gives me headaches, CloudKit or local sync? 🤔
After 3 weeks, my Discover Weekly playlist is finally on point again. 👍
Disqus integration is finally added. It's not enabled for status posts like this, only for full blog posts.
Summer ends when you install the GM builds.
What. No unread mail in my Swift evolution folder. Is the mailing list down?
And now this is the first post written on my iPhone and published with Jekyll. I'm using Workflow and Git2Go for this.
I just set up my server to build the site after any push to the Github repository. This basically enables an iOS-only workflow for me.
I just canceld my Backblaze subscription that I had for years. From now on it's Arq all the way.
Now this marks the end of my "writing in German" experiment. Back to English.
Day One unterstützt Markdown-Codeblocks und ich habe heute damit begonnen, ein kleines Entwickler-Tagebuch zu starten. Mit Code-Snippets und Erfahrungen die man gewonnen hat.
Ich finde es großartig, dass mein Safari “fa” zu http://facebook.github.io anstellen von facebook.com <facebook.com> vervollständigt.
Dieses Wochenende beschäftige ich mich das erste mal ausgiebig mit React Nativ. Alleine an Hot-Reload könnte man sich gewöhnen.

Ach ja, alle tweet-ähnlichen Einträge haben nun einen Rahmen um sie ein bisschen besser von normalen Einträgen abzugrenzen.

Update: Da der Blog nun zu Jekyll umgezogen ist, hat sich auch das Design der Seite ein wenig geändert. Keine Rahmen mehr!
Aus irgendeinem Grund habe ich heute begonnen Casey Neistat Videos anzuschauen und bisher gefällt es mir echt gut.
Wir haben die Apple Watch jetzt seit weit über einem Jahr und noch immer nur ein Watch Face mit digitaler Uhr, welches man auch ernsthaft mit Complications erweitern kann.

Smart.

![]({{ site.url }}/assets/263.jpg)
![]({{ site.url }}/assets/252.jpg)
Das goldene iPad Pro zusammen mit dem Midnight Blue Smart Cover sieht sehr sehr gut aus.
„Start a station“ von Apple Music ist auf einmal richtig gut, viel besser als Spotify Radio.
Habe meine Karte zum WWDC Beardbash bekommen! Langsam wird es aufregend!
Mein kompletter Bloggin-Prozess basiert mittlerweile auf iOS Markdown-Apps und Workflow.app. #ViticciWasRight
Mir gefällt die Idee, dass das neue Schwarz-Weiß Design der Instagram App ein Vorbote zu iOS 10 Designänderungen sein könnte.
Wow, ich liebe das neue Instagram Design. Mutig und modern!

Ach ja, die Amazon Logistics Lieferung wurde zuverlässig dort abgegeben, wo sie auch abgegeben werden sollte.

DHL/Hermes: Macht das in Zukunft auch mal so.
Ich bekomme heute das erste Mal etwas per Amazon Logistics zugestellt. Schlechter als per DHL oder Hermes kann es ja gar nicht werden.
Nach gut einem Jahr verwende ich jetzt wieder Crashlytics. Apples Lösungen sind, wie so oft in letzter Zeit, wieder nur gut gemeint.
Musste heute mal wieder auf den harten Weg lernen, dass Dropbox kein Backup ist.

In letzter Zeit habe ich immer weniger Musik gehört und ich vermute, dass es auch an schlechten Empfehlungen vom Apple Musik liegt. Ich habe dann gestern nach längerer Zeit mal wieder Discover Weekly von Spotify gehört und wirklich jeder Song war top!
Muss jetzt auch immer erstmal rechnen um zu wissen, wie alt man ist.
Ich hatte übrigens total vergessen zu erwähnen, dass ich dieses Jahr zur WWDC in San Francisco sein werde!
Hatte heute, am Sonntag, eine iOS Reviewtime von weniger als 3 Stunden. Könnte man sich dran gewöhnen.
Ich hatte heute Apple-Cinnamon Joylent und war doch positiv überrascht. Guter Geschmack und leicht cremige Konsistenz. Kann es empfehlen.
Mein Joylent ist heute nun endlich angekommen. Morgen startet dann Tag #1 des Experiments.
Mittlerweile habe ich gar keine Verwendung mehr für Wunderlist. Reminders, 2Do und Omnifocus decken nun alle Fälle ab. Fühlt sich ein bisschen so an als ginge eine Ära zu ende.
Heute ist der Tag, an dem ich mir auch Soylent/Joylent bestellt habe. Ich bin schockiert und gespannt zugleich.
Ich habe heute morgen die SSL-Zertifikate erneuert und ein paar Änderungen am CSS für diese Seite gemacht. Im Bett. Auf meinem iPhone mit Coda.

Ich bin mal wieder bei Newsify für allgemeine Nachrichten und unpersönliche Tech-Blogs gelandet. Die Inhaltsdichte bei Flipboard wird gefühlt täglich weniger und einfache RSS-Reeder können viele Artikel oft nicht schön auf einer Seite darstellen.
Viele für mich neue Apps auf meinem Homescreen:

Ich war nie ein großer Batman oder Superman Fan; nach dem Film bin ich es jetzt noch weniger.

Das beste am Film war für mich der Captain America Trailer davor.
Das iPhone 5/5s und jetzt SE Design ist noch immer mein Favorit unter allen iPhones, auch wenn es mittlerweile fast 4 Jahre alt ist.
Habe endlich die Facebook App gelöscht, nachdem sie aus unerklärlichen Grund fast 2gb Platz beansprucht hat. Zum Einloggen 2-3 die Woche reicht auch die Webapp.
… und ich bin wieder zurück bei Mail.app. Scheinbar vergesse ich alle Monate erneut, dass Mac OS und iOS bereits Mailprogramme vorinstalliert hat, die komplett ausreichend für meine Ansprüche sind.
Warum unterstützen eigentlich nicht mehr Apps das Textbundle-Format? http://textbundle.org
TXL->BUD
Versuchen die „Shake to undo“-Geste in nicht unterstützten Apps auslösen ist auch ein selten dämlicher Anblick.
Ulysses mit Workflow macht auf dem iPhone richtig Spaß!
Kostenlose Mail-Clients mit Invite-System und „IMAP coming soon“ hatten wir ja genau so schon einmal.
Im Laufe der letzten 6 Monate habe ich Sublime Text, Atom, BBEdit und Chocolat getestet und bin schließlich am Ende wieder bei Textmate 2 heraus gekommen.
Meiner Meinung nach steht Apple die Abwehrhaltung ganz und gar nicht. Daring Fireball
Instagram
Ich habe heute meine Ausgaben-App, wegen iCloud-Problemen, durch ein Numbers-Sheet ersetzt. Low tech style.
Ich wünschte Day One 2 hätte weiterhin Unterstützug für verschiedene Sync-Mechanismen. Eine proprietäre Lösung für die persönlichsten Daten fühlt sich falsch an. Ansonsten ist die App großartig.
Wie hoch stehen die Chancen, dass ein hypotetishes iPad Air 3, welches im März/April vorgestellt wird, den Apple Pencil unterstützt? Würde es zu sehr den Markt für das iPad Pro abgraben?
Ich kann The Martian, wie alle anderen auch, nur empfehlen. Super unterhaltsam und kurzweilig.
Ich sollte auch mal wieder twittern…
Ich habe endlich begonnen The Martian zu lesen, wahrscheinlich so ziemlich als letzter.
